proposed_hypotheses.text: Immune checkpoint inhibitor therapy produces shorter progression-free survival in patients with EGFR mutations.
Within this dataset, higher tumor mutational burden (TMB) is associated with lower objective response rates to immune checkpoint inhibitor monotherapy.
Drug X, treatment_x, is broadly ineffective overall but produces exceptional objective_response rates in the biomarker_z_high subgroup.

analyses.result_summary: Within biomarker_z_high=1, response rate difference is 0.55 (p<0.001).
